plants.description: A climbing aroid famous for the natural holes (fenestrations) that split its glossy leaves as they mature.
common_issues: Brown leaf edges from low humidity

plants.description: One of the most forgiving trailing vines, tolerating low light and irregular watering better than almost anything else.
Stiff, upright, sword-like leaves make this one of the most drought-tolerant plants you can own.
common_issues: Mushy base in cold, wet soil

plants.description: Arching, variegated leaves and cascades of baby "spiderettes" on long runners make this a classic hanging basket plant.
common_issues: Brown leaf tips from fluoride/chlorine in tap water
Pale leaves in too much direct sun

plants.description: Deep green leaves and elegant white "flowers" (spathes) that dramatically droop when thirsty, then perk right back up after watering.
common_issues: Brown tips from tap water minerals

plants.description: Waxy, dark green leaves grow from thick underground rhizomes that store water, making it nearly indestructible.
common_issues: Very slow growth in low light

plants.description: A spiky, gel-filled succulent long used for topical skin care, happiest on a sunny windowsill with dry spells between waterings.
common_issues: Thin, curling leaves from too little light

plants.description: Thick, glossy oval leaves on woody stems that can be shaped like a tiny tree; a classic gift for good luck.
Broad, glossy burgundy-green leaves on a plant that grows into an impressive small indoor tree.
Heart-shaped leaves on a trailing or climbing vine, one of the fastest and most reliable growers among aroids.
common_issues: Small leaves without a moss pole to climb

plants.description: Large, violin-shaped leaves make this an interior-design favorite, though it is notoriously fussy about light and being moved.
Clumping, cane-like stems and feathery fronds give a jungle feel while tolerating fairly low indoor light.
common_issues: Brown frond tips from dry air

plants.description: Slender, arching leaves with a red-edged variety, growing slowly into a striking architectural houseplant.
A weeping, small-leaved tree that is quick to drop leaves when its routine changes, but stunning once settled.
common_issues: Leaf drop after moving or drafts

plants.description: A compact, shade-tolerant palm with delicate fronds — a Victorian parlor favorite that still thrives in dim corners.
common_issues: Brown tips from dry air or salt buildup

plants.description: Large, banana-like paddle leaves on tall stems that can eventually produce dramatic orange-and-blue tropical blooms.
common_issues: No blooms without very bright light

plants.description: Glossy heart-shaped leaves and long-lasting waxy red spathes make this a favorite flowering aroid.
common_issues: Root rot in heavy, wet soil

plants.description: Thick, waxy leaves on a twining vine that rewards patience with clusters of star-shaped, fragrant porcelain flowers.
common_issues: No blooms without bright light and patience

plants.description: Glossy leaflets fan out like an umbrella from each stem on this easygoing, fast-filling indoor tree.
Grown for its swirling, iridescent patterned leaves rather than flowers, in shades of green, silver, and burgundy.
Bold, patterned leaves that visibly rise and fold at night — a "prayer plant" relative that demands humidity and consistency.
common_issues: Crispy brown edges from low humidity or tap water

plants.description: Round, coin-like leaves on thin stems, famous for producing a ring of baby plantlets around its base.
common_issues: Leggy, lopsided growth without rotating toward light

plants.description: Paper-thin, heart-shaped leaves in vivid pink, white, and green patterns, grown from a tuber that goes dormant in winter.
Patterned, painterly leaves in silver, pink, or red tones that tolerate low light better than almost any other colorful plant.
Purple, clover-shaped leaves that fold up at night and unfurl again each morning, topped with small pale flowers.
Bold leaves splashed in red, orange, and yellow that need bright light to keep their color saturated.
common_issues: Leaf drop from drafts or relocation

plants.description: Long-lasting sprays of exotic blooms grown in bark chips rather than soil, watered by soaking rather than a daily sip.
common_issues: Root rot from sitting in water
Bud drop from sudden temperature swings

plants.description: Thick scalloped leaves topped with clusters of small, long-lasting flowers in bright pinks, oranges, and yellows.
common_issues: No reblooming without a dark-night rest period

plants.description: Small rosettes of firm, striped leaves that resemble a miniature aloe — ideal for a sunny desk.
A striking rosette of stiff leaves around a central "cup" that should be kept filled with water, blooming once before producing pups.
common_issues: Rot from stagnant water in the cup

plants.description: Dense, arching fronds that crave humidity — a classic porch and bathroom plant.
Classic lobed-leaf trailing vine that climbs or cascades and tolerates cooler rooms better than most houseplants.
common_issues: Spider mites in dry, warm air

plants.description: Stiff, sword-like leaves with fine curling threads along the edges on a tough, drought-tolerant plant.
common_issues: Brown tips from fluoride in tap water

plants.description: A tree-like yucca with rigid, spine-tipped leaves atop a thickening woody trunk as it matures.
common_issues: Sharp leaf tips (place away from foot traffic)

plants.description: Dramatic arrow-shaped leaves with striking pale veins on an aroid that is picky about humidity and consistency.
Enormous heart-shaped leaves on a fast-growing tuber that can be moved outdoors as a striking patio plant in summer.
common_issues: Spider mites in dry indoor air

plants.description: Trailing strands of pea-like beads that store water, best in a hanging pot with bright light and infrequent watering.
Vividly patterned, velvety leaves in nearly any color combination, grown as an annual bedding plant or houseplant.
Fleshy, star-shaped leaf clusters topped in late summer with broad heads of tiny pink-to-rust flowers loved by pollinators.
common_issues: Flopping stems in too little sun

plants.description: Perfectly symmetrical rosettes in dusty blues, pinks, and greens — the quintessential "succulent" shape.
common_issues: Rot from water sitting on the rosette

plants.description: Glossy dark leaves and intensely fragrant white blooms, but famously demanding about humidity, acidity, and light consistency.
common_issues: Bud drop from temperature swings or dry air

plants.description: Silvery, fragrant foliage topped with purple flower spikes, needing full sun and sharp drainage to avoid rot.
A vigorous, spreading culinary herb best kept in its own pot since its runners will happily take over a garden bed.
common_issues: Becomes invasive if planted directly in the ground

plants.description: Woody, needle-leaved culinary herb with a piney fragrance that prefers to dry out fully between waterings.
common_issues: Powdery mildew in humid, still air

plants.description: A low, wiry-stemmed culinary herb that thrives on neglect, full sun, and gritty, well-drained soil.
common_issues: Woody, sparse growth without periodic trimming

plants.description: A tender, fragrant culinary annual that rewards frequent pinching with bushier growth and a longer harvest.
common_issues: Bolting (flowering) in heat, turning leaves bitter

plants.description: An assorted planter of small succulent varieties grown together — a low-maintenance starter for new plant parents.
common_issues: Uneven watering needs between varieties in one pot

plants.description: The "sensitive plant" — feathery leaflets fold instantly when touched, a favorite for teaching kids about plant movement.
common_issues: Short-lived as a houseplant (often grown as an annual)

plants.description: Round leaves and edible peppery flowers on a fast, trailing annual that actually blooms best in poor soil.
common_issues: Aphid magnet (often used as a trap crop)
Few flowers in overly rich soil

plants.description: A smaller, trailing cousin of the Monstera with delicate, deeply perforated leaves — equally happy climbing a pole or spilling from a shelf.
common_issues: Leggy vines without a pole to climb

plants.description: Deep green leaves striped with crisp white pinstripes, a compact, non-climbing philodendron bred for its variegation.
common_issues: Reverting to solid green in low light

plants.description: A heart-leaf philodendron with a bright lime-yellow stripe painted down the center of each leaf.
An electric chartreuse pothos that glows brightest in medium light and darkens slightly in lower light.
common_issues: Color dulling in very low light

plants.description: Thick, glossy, spoon-shaped leaves on a compact plant that stores water like a succulent but looks tropical.
Round, striped leaves that genuinely resemble watermelon rind, held on thin reddish stems.
common_issues: Stem rot at the base from overwatering

plants.description: Delicate heart-shaped leaves marbled in silver, trailing on thread-thin vines from small tuberous nodes.
common_issues: Thin, sparse growth in low light

plants.description: Curved, banana-shaped leaves trailing in dense strands, a quirky relative of String of Pearls.
Trailing stems densely packed with plump, blue-green beads that drop easily, so it is best handled gently.
Not a true palm — a swollen, water-storing trunk base topped with a fountain of curly, grass-like leaves.
common_issues: Root rot from overwatering the water-storing base

plants.description: Clustered slender canes topped with feathery, arching fronds that add lush volume to a bright room.
common_issues: Brown tips from low humidity or fluoride water

plants.description: Broad, deep green fronds on a stately trunk, but one that struggles indoors without high humidity and steady moisture.
True to its name, broad dark leaves survive neglect, deep shade, and temperature swings that would kill most houseplants.
common_issues: Brown streaks from direct sun scorch
Very slow to fill out a pot

plants.description: Painterly patterned leaves that fold upright at night like praying hands, then flatten again each morning.
Long, wavy-edged leaves patterned with dark blotches and a striking maroon underside.
common_issues: Crispy edges from low humidity or tap water

plants.description: Feather-like patterning on nearly translucent leaves makes this one of the showiest — and most humidity-hungry — calatheas.
Fuzzy round leaves in a neat rosette that bloom nearly year-round in clusters of purple, pink, or white flowers.
common_issues: Leaf spot from water sitting on fuzzy leaves

plants.description: A jungle cactus with flat, scalloped segments that bloom in vivid pink or red right around the holidays.
common_issues: No reblooming without a cool, dark rest period

plants.description: Flat, paddle-shaped pads covered in tiny barbed glochids (not true spines) that need careful, gloved handling.
A perfectly round, ribbed globe covered in golden spines that grows larger and more sculptural for decades.
A soil-free plant that absorbs water and nutrients through its leaves, best soaked weekly rather than potted.
common_issues: Rot if left wet without drying upside-down

plants.description: Hinged, tooth-edged traps snap shut on insects, but the plant is picky — it needs distilled water and a winter dormancy.
common_issues: Dies from tap-water minerals (needs distilled/rain water)
Traps blacken and die after several closures (normal)

plants.description: Often sold with its trunk braided, this glossy-leaved tree is said to bring good fortune to its owner.
common_issues: Yellow leaves from too much direct sun

plants.description: Soft, symmetrical tiers of needle-like branches make this a popular living alternative to a cut Christmas tree.
common_issues: Branch drop from low light or dry air

plants.description: Fast-trailing stems striped in silver and purple that root wherever a node touches soil.
Deep violet, fleshy leaves on trailing stems that keep their rich color best in bright light.
A dense, moss-like carpet of tiny round leaves that spreads quickly in a terrarium or shaded, humid pot.
An epiphytic fern mounted on wood or bark, with antler-like fronds that fan out dramatically as it matures.
common_issues: Rot from watering the mounting board too often

plants.description: Delicate, fan-shaped leaflets on wiry black stems — beautiful but unforgiving if the soil ever fully dries out.
common_issues: Fronds collapse fast if allowed to dry out

plants.description: Wavy, undivided fronds unfurl from a central rosette that resembles a bird's nest, unusual among ferns.
common_issues: Rot from water sitting in the central crown

plants.description: Leaves speckled with pink, red, or white spots, best kept compact with regular pinching before it flowers and fades.
Small leaves etched with a network of white or pink veins, dramatic enough to visibly wilt within hours of drying out.
common_issues: Dramatic wilting when soil dries (usually recovers after watering)

plants.description: Bold, strappy leaves in shades of pink, red, and purple on a slender cane, traditionally planted for good luck.
common_issues: Brown tips from fluoride in tap water

plants.description: Velvety, silver-splashed heart-shaped leaves on a trailing vine that is not a true pothos but is cared for the same way.
common_issues: Losing silver flecking in low light

plants.description: Arrow-shaped leaves that change shape as the plant matures, growing bushy when young and vining as it ages.
Thorny vines wrapped in papery, vividly colored bracts that only bloom heavily with hours of direct sun.
common_issues: Few blooms without very bright light
Leaf drop when moved or underwatered

plants.description: Large, trumpet-shaped blooms that each last only a day or two but are produced continuously in bright light.
common_issues: Bud drop from being moved or underwatered

plants.description: A twining vine that bursts into intensely fragrant white star-shaped flowers, needing a cool winter rest to rebloom.
common_issues: No blooms without a cool winter rest period

plants.description: Rounded, scalloped leaves and clusters of long-blooming flowers in reds, pinks, and whites, best in full sun.
Trumpet-shaped, ruffled flowers in nearly every color, blooming non-stop through summer with regular feeding.
common_issues: Sticky sap on leaves (normal, not a pest)

plants.description: Ruffled orange and yellow blooms with a sharp, pungent scent that is said to repel garden pests.
A fast-growing culinary herb that bolts (flowers and turns bitter) quickly in heat, so cooler conditions extend the harvest.
common_issues: Bolting to seed in warm weather
Short lifespan even with good care

plants.description: Thin, hollow, onion-flavored leaves that regrow quickly after cutting, topped with edible purple pom-pom flowers.
common_issues: Fewer new shoots without regular harvesting

plants.description: A low, spreading Mediterranean herb that develops the most flavor when kept a little on the dry side in full sun.
Glossy, fragrant leaves and fragrant blossoms that can develop into real lemons with enough direct sun.
common_issues: Leaf drop from underwatering or cold drafts

plants.description: Large lobed leaves on a deciduous tree that drops its leaves in winter dormancy — expected, not a sign of trouble.
common_issues: Fruit failing to ripen without enough summer sun

plants.description: A classic beginner bonsai species, trained into a miniature tree shape and needing frequent, careful watering in its shallow pot.
common_issues: Drying out fast in a shallow bonsai pot

plants.description: Not a true bamboo, but a Dracaena often grown in a vase of water and twisted into decorative stalks for good fortune.
common_issues: Yellowing stalks from chlorinated tap water (use filtered/distilled)

plants.description: Heart-shaped marbled leaves and swept-back winter blooms that prefer cool rooms and go dormant in summer heat.
common_issues: Rot from watering the tuber crown directly

plants.description: Velvety, near-black leaves etched with brilliant white veins, a collector-grade aroid that demands high humidity.
common_issues: Root rot in dense, wet soil
